← all tutorials

Distributed Locking — ZooKeeper / Redis / Redlock / Fencing — interactive 📖 DISTRIBUTED_LOCKING.md 📄 distributed_locking.py

📖 Full guide (.md, green) — the long-form written explanation.  📄 Source code (.py, orange) — the ground-truth logic this page is verified against.
A lock is a talking stick: only the holder may write. The question is what guarantees the intermediary gives you. Consensus-based (ZooKeeper) locks are safe by construction (the holder is the unique minimum — no clocks). Clock-based locks (Redis/Redlock) break when a holder freezes in a GC pause longer than the TTL. Fencing tokens restore safety at the storage. All numbers recompute live in JS from the identical models as distributed_locking.py.

① ZooKeeper ephemeral lock (naive) — one release wakes the whole HERD

What to do: C1 holds /lock; every other client has a watch on it. Hit release C1: ZK deletes the ephemeral node and fires the watch on ALL N−1 waiters at once — the thundering herd. (The fix is in panel ②.)
C1 holds the lock

② ZooKeeper sequential nodes — the ordered QUEUE (one wakeup per release)

What to do: each client creates /lock-NNN; the lowest number holds the lock and each client watches only its predecessor. Hit step release: exactly ONE client wakes (the successor). Holder = argmin_seq → unique → mutual exclusion by construction.
queue: 3 clients waiting

③ Redis SETNX — SET NX PX, and the stale holder under a GC pause

What to do: C1 acquires with SET lock c1 NX PX <ttl> at tick 0, then FREEZES. Drag the GC pause past the remaining TTL: the lock expires underneath C1, C2 grabs it, and now |holders| = 2. Mutual exclusion is violated — clocks, not crashes, are the culprit.
C1 works ticks 0→10, then freezes

④ Redlock — 5 masters, majority quorum, and two DISJOINT majorities

What to do: C1 acquires majority {M1,M2,M3}. Hit clock jump on M3 (an NTP step): C1's lock on M3 expires "early". C2 then assembles a different majority {M3,M4,M5} — both are valid quorums, so two holders. Majority does not fix bad clocks.
N=5, quorum=floor(5/2)+1=3, TTL=10

⑤ Fencing tokens — the storage rejects the stale holder

What to do: press play. Each grant carries a monotonic token (33, then 34). C2's write (token 34) is accepted; when frozen C1 wakes and writes with its stale token 33, the storage rejects it (33 ≤ 34). The data is never corrupted.
step 0 / 4

⑥ Gold — consensus ⟹ ≤1 holder every tick; clock-based does not

Recomputed live in JS, mirroring distributed_locking.py GOLD CHECK: the 10-tick ZK-sequential timeline (consensus) keeps |holders| = 1 at every tick; the Redis SETNX + GC-pause counter-model reaches 2.
check: … JS recompute matches distributed_locking.py GOLD: consensus max|holders|=1 (every tick), redis max|holders|=2, fencing C1(33) rejected / C2(34) accepted.
